actions.description: Create a new bearer token.
List bearer tokens for the current member.
Revoke a bearer token.

actions.authorizationNote: Requires at least one active membership in any club. Must not already be a member of the target club. Capped at 3 pending cross-applications across all clubs.

businessErrors.meaning: Only active members of an existing club can use the cross-club apply path.
businessErrors.recovery: Use the cold public admissions path, or authenticate as a member with an active membership.

businessErrors.meaning: The actor already has a membership record in the target club.
businessErrors.recovery: Stop retrying this path and contact the club admin instead.

businessErrors.meaning: The actor already has a pending admission for the target club.
businessErrors.recovery: Wait for the current admission to be resolved before retrying.

businessErrors.meaning: The actor has too many pending cross-club applications across the network.
businessErrors.recovery: Wait for existing applications to resolve before applying to more clubs.

businessErrors.meaning: The actor profile is missing the required name or email for cross-application.
businessErrors.recovery: Update the profile first, then request a fresh challenge.

actions.description: Request a reduced-difficulty PoW challenge for an existing network member applying to a new club. The response carries the club's admission policy and an expiresAt timestamp; the challenge is valid for one hour from creation. Read the policy before drafting — the admission gate is a literal completeness check, the same one cold applicants face.

clubSlug.description: Slug of the club to apply to

admissionPolicy.description: The club's admission policy. Treat this as the literal completeness checklist your application must satisfy: the gate only verifies that every explicit ask is answered, not fit or quality.

difficulty.description: Canonical difficulty: the number of trailing hex zeros required on sha256(challengeId + ":" + nonce). Use this value when solving — do not assume a constant.

expiresAt.description: ISO timestamp when this challenge expires. The countdown starts at challenge creation, not after the puzzle is solved; there is no separate post-solve resubmission window.

maxAttempts.description: Total submissions allowed against this challenge before it is consumed.

businessErrors.meaning: The challenge ID does not exist or is no longer available.
businessErrors.recovery: Request a fresh challenge and try again.

businessErrors.meaning: The challenge was issued to a different authenticated member.
businessErrors.recovery: Use the token that requested the challenge, or request a new one under the current account.

businessErrors.meaning: The challenge expired before submission.
businessErrors.recovery: Request a fresh challenge.

businessErrors.meaning: The proof-of-work nonce did not satisfy the challenge difficulty.
businessErrors.recovery: Re-solve the PoW for this challenge and resubmit.

businessErrors.meaning: A concurrent request already consumed this challenge.
businessErrors.recovery: Request a fresh challenge and try again.

businessErrors.meaning: The actor profile is missing the required name or email for cross-application.
businessErrors.recovery: Update the profile first, then request a fresh challenge.

businessErrors.meaning: The admission gate is temporarily unavailable.
businessErrors.recovery: Retry the same submission after a short delay. If the outage persists, surface it to the user.

actions.description: Submit a solved cross-apply PoW challenge with an application. Name and email are locked to your profile. On needs_revision the challenge is not consumed — patch only the items in feedback and resubmit against the same challengeId.

application.description: Your application. The admission gate is a literal completeness check: it rejects when an explicit ask in the policy is left unanswered, but does not reject for vagueness, brevity, or quality on its own. If the policy is question-shaped, answer every question directly.

nonce.description: Canonical PoW: a nonce such that sha256(challengeId + ":" + nonce) ends in `difficulty` hex zeros. The server currently also accepts a leading-zero compatibility fallback, but clients should solve for trailing zeros.

notes: Name and email are locked to the current profile. Do not send them again on submit.
A needs_revision status means the challenge remains valid and the same challengeId and nonce can be reused.
When status is accepted, the message field is the server's canonical response text.

attemptsRemaining.description: Remaining submissions against the same challenge. The challenge is not consumed by needs_revision and remains valid until expiry or attempt exhaustion; reuse the same challengeId and nonce unless the server explicitly returns invalid_proof.

feedback.description: Revision brief from the admission gate. Treat as a literal list of gaps to fix — patch only the items it identifies, do not redraft the application from scratch. Receiving this means the PoW was accepted and the content needs revision.

actions.description: Read your own admission records, including your application text, status, club, and timestamps.

Request a proof-of-work challenge bound to a specific club. The response carries the club's admission policy and an expiresAt timestamp; the challenge is valid for one hour from creation. Read the policy before drafting — the admission gate is a literal completeness check.

clubSlug.description: Slug of the club to apply to

admissionPolicy.description: The club's admission policy. Treat this as the literal completeness checklist your application must satisfy: the gate only verifies that every explicit ask is answered, not fit or quality.

difficulty.description: Canonical difficulty: the number of trailing hex zeros required on sha256(challengeId + ":" + nonce). Use this value when solving — do not assume a constant.

expiresAt.description: ISO timestamp when this challenge expires. The countdown starts at challenge creation, not after the puzzle is solved; there is no separate post-solve resubmission window.

maxAttempts.description: Total submissions allowed against this challenge before it is consumed.

businessErrors.meaning: The challenge ID does not exist or is no longer available.
businessErrors.recovery: Request a fresh challenge and try again.

businessErrors.meaning: The challenge was issued for the authenticated cross-club path, not the cold public path.
businessErrors.recovery: Use admissions.crossClub.submitApplication with the authenticated member token instead.

businessErrors.meaning: The challenge expired before submission.
businessErrors.recovery: Request a fresh challenge.

businessErrors.meaning: The proof-of-work nonce did not satisfy the challenge difficulty.
businessErrors.recovery: Re-solve the PoW for this challenge and resubmit.

businessErrors.meaning: A concurrent request already consumed this challenge.
businessErrors.recovery: Request a fresh challenge and try again.

businessErrors.meaning: The admission gate is temporarily unavailable.
businessErrors.recovery: Retry the same submission after a short delay. If the outage persists, surface it to the user.

actions.description: Submit a solved proof-of-work challenge with an application. On needs_revision the challenge is not consumed — patch only the items in feedback and resubmit against the same challengeId.

application.description: Your application. The admission gate is a literal completeness check: it rejects when an explicit ask in the policy is left unanswered, but does not reject for vagueness, brevity, or quality on its own. If the policy is question-shaped, answer every question directly.

email.description: Email address (must contain @, max 500 chars). Server trims and lowercases.

name.description: Full name (first and last name required, max 500 chars). Server trims whitespace.

nonce.description: Canonical PoW: a nonce such that sha256(challengeId + ":" + nonce) ends in `difficulty` hex zeros. The server currently also accepts a leading-zero compatibility fallback, but clients should solve for trailing zeros.

notes: The club is bound to the challenge. Do not send clubSlug again on submit.
Use application for the free-text field; do not send reason.
A needs_revision status means the challenge remains valid and the same challengeId and nonce can be reused.
When status is accepted, the message field is the server's canonical response text.

attemptsRemaining.description: Remaining submissions against the same challenge. The challenge is not consumed by needs_revision and remains valid until expiry or attempt exhaustion; reuse the same challengeId and nonce unless the server explicitly returns invalid_proof.

feedback.description: Revision brief from the admission gate. Treat as a literal list of gaps to fix — patch only the items it identifies, do not redraft the application from scratch. Receiving this means the PoW was accepted and the content needs revision.

businessErrors.meaning: The submission was rejected for soliciting or facilitating clearly illegal activity.
businessErrors.recovery: Relay the reason to the user, revise the sponsorship text, and resubmit.

businessErrors.meaning: The content gate returned a non-passing verdict after schema validation.
businessErrors.recovery: Review the feedback, revise the sponsorship text, and resubmit.

businessErrors.meaning: The content gate is temporarily unavailable.
businessErrors.recovery: Retry after a short delay. If the problem persists, surface the outage to the user.

actions.description: Send a direct message to another member. Requires at least one shared club, or an existing thread between the participants.

clientKey.description: Idempotency key — same key with same payload returns the original message; same key with different payload returns 409 client_key_conflict

notes: clientKey is scoped per sender globally, not per thread.
scopeRules: DMs are not club-scoped. Do not send clubId when calling messages.send.
A shared club is only required to start a new thread; existing threads remain replyable even if shared clubs later drop to zero.

actions.description: List a member's visible profiles across clubs. Omit memberId for the current actor.

clubId.description: Restrict to one club inside the actor scope.

memberId.description: Target member ID. Omit for own profile.

actions.description: Update the current actor club-scoped profile fields for one club.

notes: Use members.updateIdentity for global identity fields like handle and displayName. profile.update only changes club-scoped profile fields.

actions.description: Get quota usage for the current member across accessible clubs.
notes: This returns the effective per-club limit after applying any club-specific override and the clubadmin/owner 3x multiplier.

actions.description: Resolve the current member session, accessible clubs, and any pending update context.
notes: Useful session information lives in the response envelope's actor block, not in data.

actions.description: List bearer tokens for a specific member.
Revoke a bearer token for a specific member.
Transition a membership from payment_pending to active and create a subscription row. Idempotent: no-op if already active with same or later paidThrough.

paidThrough.description: ISO 8601 date/datetime for subscription period end

actions.description: Archive a club, bypassing the paid-club guard. Idempotent: no-op if already archived.
Ban a member: sets member state to banned, transitions all non-terminal memberships to banned, and ends all live subscriptions. Idempotent: no-op if already banned.
Transition active membership to cancelled. Does not end the subscription (Stripe keeps it active until period end). Idempotent: no-op if already cancelled.

membershipId.description: Membership to cancel at period end

actions.description: Transition a membership to expired from active, renewal_pending, cancelled, or payment_pending. Sets subscription to ended. Idempotent: no-op if already expired.
Transition active membership to renewal_pending and mark subscription past_due. Idempotent: no-op if already renewal_pending.
Extend a subscription period end date forward. If membership was cancelled, transitions back to active. Idempotent: no-op if already at or past the given date.

newPaidThrough.description: New ISO 8601 period end date (must be forward-only)

actions.description: Set the membership price for a club by creating a new club version. Pass amount=null to make the club free. Idempotent: no-op if price already matches.

amount.description: Price amount in minor or major units, or null to clear

notes: Use after="latest" to skip backlog and start from the current stream tip. The server resolves it to the real cursor before listing.

businessErrors.meaning: A member cannot vouch for themselves.
businessErrors.recovery: Do not retry this request.

businessErrors.meaning: The actor has already vouched for this member in this club.
businessErrors.recovery: Treat the existing vouch as canonical instead of retrying.

transportErrorCodes.meaning: The client cached an older schema. Refetch /api/schema and /skill, then retry.

updates.acknowledgment: Acknowledge inbox-sourced updates via updates.acknowledge. Activity updates advance via cursor.

stream.note: Browser EventSource cannot set Authorization headers; use fetch with a streaming reader.

after.description: Opaque cursor or "latest". Falls back to Last-Event-ID header if omitted.

limit.description: Max updates per poll cycle inside the stream (1-20).

resumeHeaders.Last-Event-ID: Set automatically by EventSource on reconnect. Used as `after` cursor when query param is absent.

stream.sseIdBehavior: The compound cursor is attached as `id:` on the last update event in each batch, enabling automatic resume via Last-Event-ID.
